exam_name: IBPS RRB PO (Officer Scale-I) - CRP RRB XV

subjects.name: Reasoning Ability (Prelims & Mains)
subjects.analysis: The backbone of RRB PO. Prelims focuses on speed puzzles, while Mains demands high-level logical reasoning and input-output skills.

subjects.name: Quantitative Aptitude / Numerical Ability
subjects.analysis: No English in Prelims makes Quant highly competitive. Accuracy in DI and Arithmetic determines the state-wise cutoff clearance.

subjects.name: General Awareness (Financial Focus)
subjects.analysis: Specifically targets Rural Banking, NABARD, and Government Schemes for the agricultural sector.

topics.name: Banking & Financial Awareness
topics.sub_topics: RBI, NABARD, RRB History, PSL Norms

subjects.name: Language (English OR Hindi)
subjects.analysis: Unique to RRB: Candidates can choose between English or Hindi. Both test grammar and comprehension.

subjects.name: Computer Knowledge
subjects.analysis: Low marks per question (0.5) but high impact on total score. Focus on practical office software and security.

mentorship_data.title: Prelims: The 45-Minute Blitz
mentorship_data.content: Since there is no English in Prelims, the cutoff goes exceptionally high (up to 60+ out of 80). You must attempt 65+ with 100% accuracy. Focus on fast calculation.

mentorship_data.title: Mains: Composite Time Logic
mentorship_data.content: Even with sectional timing, you must manage the switch between Hindi/English and GA effectively. Computer and GA should be finished in 30 mins total.

mentorship_data.title: Regional Language Proficiency
mentorship_data.content: Crucial for RRBs. You must be proficient in the local language of the state you are applying for. Proficiency will be checked during DV/Interview.

mentorship_data.title: Final Selection Ratio
mentorship_data.content: Final merit is calculated in an 80:20 ratio between Mains marks and Interview marks. Prelims is only a screening round.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is there a sectional cutoff in RRB PO?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Yes, you must clear both the sectional and the overall cutoff in both Prelims and Mains.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Can I choose Hindi instead of English?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Yes, RRB allows you to choose either English or Hindi for the language section in the Mains exam.

frequently_asked_questions.q: What is the age limit for Officer Scale I?
frequently_asked_questions.a: The general age limit is usually between 18 and 30 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories).

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is there an interview for RRB Clerk?
frequently_asked_questions.a: No, there is no interview for RRB Clerk (Office Assistant), only for RRB PO (Officer Scale I, II, III).
Standard penalty of 0.25 (1/4th) of the marks assigned to that question is applied for every wrong answer.

frequently_asked_questions.q: What is the salary of an RRB PO?
frequently_asked_questions.a: The starting basic pay is approximately ₹48,480, with total in-hand salary ranging from ₹65,000 to ₹75,000 including allowances.
